req: Explicit and unreserved statement of compliance with IFRS for SMEs is made in the notes.
Statements present fairly financial position, performance and cash flows; any departure disclosed with reason.
Going concern assessed; material uncertainties casting significant doubt disclosed; if not a going concern, the fact, basis used and reason disclosed.
Reports at least annually; if the period differs from one year, the period covered, the reason, and the fact comparatives are not fully comparable are disclosed.
Presentation and classification of items is consistent from one period to the next.
Where comparatives are reclassified, the nature, amount and reason are disclosed (or the reason if reclassification is impracticable).
Comparative (prior-period) information is presented for all amounts in every statement AND every note, including narrative where relevant.
Each material class of similar items is presented separately; dissimilar items are not aggregated unless individually immaterial.
Complete set presented: statement of financial position; statement of comprehensive income; statement of changes in equity; statement of cash flows; and notes.
If a statement of income & retained earnings replaces the SOCI + SOCE, this is used only where the only equity movements are profit/loss, dividends, prior-period corrections and policy changes.
Each statement and the notes are clearly identified and distinguished from other information.
Each statement header shows: entity name; whether individual or consolidated; reporting date/period covered; presentation currency; and level of rounding.

Face presents all applicable minimum line items (cash & equivalents; trade & other receivables; financial assets; inventories; PPE; investment property; intangibles; biological assets; investments; trade & other payables; financial liabilities; current tax; deferred tax; provisions; equity).
A deferred tax asset/liability line is presented where temporary differences exist — IFRS for SMEs has NO exemption from deferred tax.
Additional line items, headings and subtotals are presented where relevant to understanding.
A current/non-current split is presented (or a liquidity ordering where more relevant and reliable); classification criteria correctly applied.
Deferred tax is classified as non-current (never current).
Sub-classifications of the line items are disclosed on the face or in the notes (e.g., classes of PPE, breakdown of receivables/payables).
Share capital & reserves: number of shares authorised, issued and fully paid; par value; reconciliation of shares outstanding; rights & restrictions; and a description of each reserve.
An entity without share capital discloses equivalent information for each category of equity.
Total comprehensive income is presented either in a single statement or in two statements, and the chosen approach is applied consistently.

Face presents applicable minimum line items: revenue; finance costs; share of associates/JV results; tax expense; a single amount for discontinued operations; profit or loss; each item of OCI; total comprehensive income.
Finance costs are presented as a separate line where borrowings or other financing exist.
Material items of income and expense are disclosed separately (nature and amount).
No item of income or expense is presented as 'extraordinary'.
Expenses are analysed by nature or by function; if by function, depreciation, amortisation and employee benefits expense are disclosed in the notes.
For each component of equity, a reconciliation from opening to closing carrying amount is presented.
Total comprehensive income for the period and the effects of any retrospective application/restatement are shown for each component.
Transactions with owners are shown separately — contributions, distributions/dividends, and changes in ownership interests.
Prior-period reconciliation is presented (both years reconciled, not the current year only).
Dividends recognised as distributions to owners are disclosed (amount and per-share) on the face or in the notes.
Cash flows are classified into operating, investing and financing activities.
Operating cash flows are presented by the direct or the indirect method.
Major classes of gross cash receipts and payments in investing and financing are shown separately (no netting except where permitted).
Interest and dividends paid and received are each disclosed separately and classified consistently period to period.
Cash flows for income taxes are disclosed separately and classified as operating unless specifically identified with financing/investing.
Material non-cash investing/financing transactions are excluded from the statement and disclosed elsewhere.
The components of cash & cash equivalents are disclosed and reconciled to the equivalent items on the SOFP.
Significant cash & cash equivalent balances not available for use are disclosed.
The prior-period comparative column of the cash flow statement is populated.
Notes present the basis of preparation, the accounting policies applied, information required by IFRS for SMEs not shown elsewhere, and information relevant to understanding.
Notes are presented in a systematic order and each item on the face of the statements is cross-referenced to its note.
Notes follow the normal order: (a) compliance statement; (b) summary of significant accounting policies; (c) supporting information in statement/line order; (d) other disclosures.
The significant judgements management made in applying the accounting policies are disclosed.
Key sources of estimation uncertainty are disclosed — their nature and the carrying amounts of the assets/liabilities affected.
The measurement basis(es) used and each significant accounting policy relevant to understanding are disclosed.
The accounting policy (measurement basis) for financial instruments is disclosed.
The carrying amounts of each category of financial asset and financial liability are disclosed, on the face or in the notes.
Terms and conditions of financial instruments that are material to the entity are disclosed.
Defaults and breaches on loans payable during the period are disclosed.
Items of income, expense, gains and losses on financial instruments (e.g., interest income/expense) are disclosed.
Inventory policy (cost formula) is disclosed; the carrying amount and classifications, the amount recognised as expense, and any write-downs/reversals are disclosed.
For each class of PPE: measurement basis, depreciation method, useful lives/rates, gross carrying amount and accumulated depreciation (opening & closing), and a reconciliation of movements.
Restrictions on title / assets pledged, and contractual commitments to acquire PPE, are disclosed.

For each class of intangible: useful lives or amortisation rates, method, the line in which amortisation is included, gross carrying amount and accumulated amortisation, and a reconciliation — presented SEPARATELY from PPE.
For each class of provision: a reconciliation of the carrying amount, and the nature, timing and uncertainties of the outflows.
Contingent liabilities and contingent assets are disclosed.
The revenue recognition policy and the categories of revenue recognised in the period are disclosed.
Impairment losses and reversals recognised in the period are disclosed by class of asset.
Employee benefit disclosures are made (defined-contribution amount recognised; defined-benefit plan disclosures where applicable).
The components of tax expense are disclosed separately (current tax; deferred tax).
A reconciliation of tax expense to accounting profit multiplied by the applicable rate (numerical reconciliation) is disclosed.
Deferred tax is analysed by category of temporary difference; changes in applicable tax rates are explained.
The date the financial statements were authorised for issue and who gave that authorisation are disclosed.
Material non-adjusting events after the reporting date are disclosed — their nature and an estimate of the financial effect.
The name of the entity's parent and its ultimate controlling party are disclosed, regardless of whether transactions occurred.
Key management personnel compensation is disclosed in aggregate.
Related party transactions and outstanding balances are disclosed: nature of the relationship, amount, balances, terms, and any provisions for doubtful debts.
